publicationStmt.publisher: Die Schriftkultur des christlichen Äthiopiens und Eritreas: Eine multimediale Forschungsumgebung / Beta maṣāḥǝft

availability.licence: This file is licensed under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0.

msItem.incipit: A supplication for . The name of an initial supplicant is erased and the new one is written over in a different red ink.

msItem.explicit: A supplication for .

support.note: The hole in has been sewn up.

supportDesc.foliation: Foliation is provided by the library and is marked in pencil on the upper right edge of the recto folia.
supportDesc.condition: Despite some brownish stains (e.g., on ) and the general dirtiness of the parchment, the text is fully readable and both the parchment and the cover are in a very good condition.

layout.ab: The upper line is written above the ruling.
The bottom line is written above the ruling.

handNote.seg: Divine names, incipits, punctuation marks, Ethiopic numerals anf the supplicant’s name.

decoNote.desc: Lines of black and red dots at the end of almost each text part, e.g., on .
A miniature of the art theme with three episodes, depicting , and an .
A miniature depicting healing the priest, who has hurt his back while bowing before Her.
A miniature depicting speaking to a deceased priest, while two angels are waiting to take his sioul to Paradise.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting the scribe Damianus writing the name of with gold ink.
A miniature depicting appearing to Damianus on his death bed.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting ʾAbbās, the bishop of Rome, touching the face of a woman during the Holy Mysteries.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting forgiving ʾAbbās, the bishop of Rome, and rejoining his hand.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting Yǝsḥaq prostrating before .
A miniature of the art theme with three episodes.
A miniature of the art theme .
A miniature depicting Zacharias praying to .
A miniature of the art theme , depicting a sick man being baptized in the river Jordan by .
A miniature of the art theme , depicting commanding to two angels to help Sophia, abbess of Mount Carmel, to deliver a child and to show it to the man called Felix.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting two episodes of the miracle. In the first of them, Sophia stands before the bishop, and in another one, Felix with her child is depicted before the Bishop.
A miniature of the art theme with three episodes.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting consoling sick .
A miniature of the art theme , depicting monks beating their brethren.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting , a praying monk and two angels.
A miniature of the art theme , consisting of three episodes.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting Our Lord Jesus Christ in glory, protecting the cannibal’s soul from being carried off to hell by a , and an archangel weighing water and souls.
A miniature depicting the story of and his rescue.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting healing the Greek merchant who had got an arrow in his eye.
A miniature depicting a blind girl and her mother praying to , and two angels.
A miniature depicting restoring the sight of the blind girl with her breast milk.
A miniature of the art theme with four episodes.
A miniature of the , depicting two scribes falling into sin while copying the Book of the Miracles of .
A miniature of the art theme , depicting delievering the souls of two sinned scribes to two demons.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting under the guise of a dove rescues from the prison.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting a church guard beating the priest called Kaṭir.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting answering to the prayers of Kaṭir and an archangel punishing the church guard with paralisys.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting the story of Nazib’s imprisonment.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting a story of Nazib’s release, the , and .
A miniature depicting a formely lame person telling his healing story to the Archbishop so that the later stops being angry with another lame man praying for healing before the icon of .
A miniature of the art theme , depicting healing the leprous Bishop Mercurius.
A miniature depicting either Zacharias or Mercurius.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting healing a woman with a broken foot.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting the robbers becoming monks after having witnessed Zakāryās speaking to .
A miniature of the art theme , depicting the story of two thieves, whose stolen bread turned into stones.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting Yolyānā, Barbārā, and two thieves standing before and two angels.
A miniature depicting the story of three men suffering a shipwreck, two of whom were rescued by , and the third one being punished for mocking Her.
A miniature depicting two Arabs making offerings to a Monastery.
A miniature depicting appearing to a priest in his dream and ordering him to build a monastery in her name.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting the church of ʿƎqonā being transferred by the power of .
A miniature of the art theme , depicting restoring the sight of a blind priest with her breast milk.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting a Greek merchant getting wounded in his eye by an arrow.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting the woman describing the miracle of being rescued by after having been abandoned by companion pilgrims during their journey to the shrine.
A miniature of the art theme , depicting giving a thirsty dog water from her shoe, surrounded by two angels.
A miniature depicting two companions of , one of whom is drawing water and another one is driving away a thirsty dog from the water jars.

item.desc: ዘአትለውኪያ፡ ማርያም፡ is written by the same hand that added the name of to supplications.
The ownership note (№2, This manuscript is the property of Lady Meux) is written in pencil first and then otlined in black ink.
The title, ተአምረ፡ ማርያም፡ ዘቅዱስ፡ መድኃኔ፡ ዓለም፡, is written in a brownish ink.
The space for a supplicant’s name was left blank.

binding.decoNote: Brown tooled leather cover with colourful textile inlays with a floral pattern. The blind tooled ornament includes crosses, double line fillet, double circles, and other ornamental elements.
Endbands are present, the headband is slightly damaged.

history.provenance: The manuscript contains numerous supplications for and . The name of the latter has been added later by another hand in a different red colour. As it is evident from the ownership note, the manuscript belonged at some point to Lady Valeria Susie Meux of Theobald's Park, Waltham Cross, Essex. After her death in 1911 the whole collection was sold. Many of the objects including this manuscript were bought by William Randolph Hearst, whose collection was sold at Sotheby's, 11-12 July 1939.

bibl.note: This is the manuscript used by Budge in his edition; the majority of the miniatures of the manuscript were also reprinted.

projectDesc.p: Encoded according to TEI P5 Guidelines.

revisionDesc.change: Updated content, added physical description in accordance to the digital copy.
